rationale: The cycle-1 rev-10 plan for Lachesis successfully resolves previous architectural objections and is implementation-ready as-is. It achieves strict boundary enforcement through a static import oracle explicitly allowing only the sanctioned merkle-dag vendor module while failing closed on all dynamic or out-of-profile imports. The trust model is rigorously defined as conditional on the external CURRENT-AUTHORITY root, adopting honest non-claims to avoid falsely asserting cryptographic authorship or re-deriving Clotho content addresses. Furthermore, separating operational data from institutional memory records establishes a clean, closed-set validation flow, while shifting from presence-based completeness to explicit, authority-anchored coverage attestations closes a significant semantic gap. Per the pre-review constraints, the enrollment-flip expectation change remains held for a separate Eye ruling.
